design_intent: Focus20-first repair rules for the hardv3 release-grounded Linkding websites. These rules keep the compact V2.4 scope but make staged checkpoint continuation, post-login redirect trust, and invisible-navigation recovery more executable for Qwen3-VL.

rules.title: After one invisible or timed-out click, never click the same bid again

trigger.old_assumption: If a hidden or stale target timed out once, repeating the same bid may eventually work.
observed_symptoms: The last click failed because the element is not visible, not stable, timed out, or could not be found.
The URL is unchanged and the same bid or target is being retried.

required_observations: Read the last_action_error and compare the current URL with the previous step before acting again.
Look for one different visible semantic target, a visible opener, or a same-site route fallback instead of the same hidden bid.
adaptation_strategy: After one invisible or timed-out click, never click the same bid again in this task.
Switch immediately to one visible opener for that navigation group, or to direct local route fallback for navigation-only tasks.
Reassess after that one different action instead of repeating the hidden target.
forbidden_actions: Do not spend multiple steps repeating the same invisible menu or stale bid click.
Do not trust DOM presence of a hidden target as proof it is human-clickable.
verification_check: The next action after the timeout uses a different visible target, opener, fallback, or final answer.
Recovery changes menu visibility, URL, or reveals the requested state.

support.evidence: Focus20 hardv3 lost rows repeatedly retried invisible or stale targets, especially structural 1601306 and related stuck navigation traces.

rules.title: Use direct local route fallback once, then reassess for a checkpoint or final state

trigger.old_assumption: The agent must keep searching for a drifted nav affordance even when the same-site route is obvious.
observed_symptoms: The task is navigation-only or asks only to land on a page or form.
A visible navigation attempt failed once or the page remains stuck on the wrong same-site page.

required_observations: Confirm the task only asks to open, land on, or move to a same-site destination rather than save new data.
After route fallback loads, immediately inspect whether the page is already final or is a staged checkpoint with one visible continuation control.
adaptation_strategy: After one failed nav attempt, use one direct local route fallback such as /settings, /tags, /bookmarks, or /bookmarks/new on the current origin.
Once that route is loaded, do not navigate again immediately; first check for a visible review/apply/open/continue control or for final evidence.
Finalize if the goal evidence is already visible, otherwise click one visible continuation control and reassess.
forbidden_actions: Do not chain multiple same-site gotos without reassessing the new page.
Do not use route fallback for tasks that require saving or editing data before completion.
verification_check: The URL path matches the requested destination before any further navigation.
The next step after route fallback is either a continuation click or finalization.

support.evidence: Hardv3 lost rows often reached the correct route, then wasted steps on noop or repeated goto instead of acting on the staged page.

rules.title: Treat staged review or apply pages as one-click checkpoints, not as noop states

trigger.old_assumption: If the destination route is already correct, waiting with noop is safer than interacting with the checkpoint page.
observed_symptoms: The current route already matches the task destination, but the page shows a visible Review, Apply, Open, Continue, or similar control.
Final evaluator text, editable fields, or committed filter evidence are not yet visible.

required_observations: Check whether the current page is a staged checkpoint rather than the final evidence state.
If a single visible continuation control is present, use it once before considering noop or any new navigation.
adaptation_strategy: Click one visible continuation control that semantically advances the staged page, such as review, apply, open, continue, or a visible subpanel opener.
After that one continuation click, reassess the URL, headings, visible fields, and required evidence.
If the goal evidence is now visible, finalize immediately instead of waiting.
forbidden_actions: Do not noop repeatedly on a staged checkpoint page.
Do not abandon the correct destination route before trying the visible continuation.
verification_check: The continuation click reveals fields, query evidence, or the requested destination state.
Long noop loops disappear after destination arrival.

support.evidence: Focus20 hardv3 lost rows on /bookmarks/new and related destinations reached the right route but then stalled in staged checkpoint pages.

rules.title: After login redirect success, never return to /login and operate on the redirected page

trigger.old_assumption: If a login task still feels incomplete, revisiting login?next=... is an acceptable recovery step.
observed_symptoms: The submit click succeeded and the current URL changed away from /login to the same-site destination route.
A staged card, review page, or destination page is already open after login.

required_observations: Check whether the current URL is still /login or has already changed to the destination route.
If the route changed away from /login, inspect the redirected page for visible continuation controls or final evidence before taking any new navigation action.
adaptation_strategy: Once the route changed away from /login, never goto('/login?...') again in that task.
Operate on the redirected page: either click one visible continuation control or finalize if the goal evidence is already visible.
Preserve the task-specific next parameters and redirected form or query values instead of replaying generic login recovery.
forbidden_actions: Do not navigate back to /login after a successful redirect.
Do not oscillate between the redirected page and login.
verification_check: The trace remains on the redirected destination after login.
The task finishes from the redirected page rather than from another login attempt.

support.evidence: Hardv3 Focus20 login-to-form and login-to-filter failures repeatedly returned to login?next=... even after redirect success.

rules.title: Open collapsed navigation only through a visible opener, then retry once or fall back

trigger.old_assumption: A hidden or regrouped navigation item can be activated directly because it appears somewhere in the page structure.
observed_symptoms: A destination like Settings, Tags, or Bookmarks is present but the target click reports not visible.
Navigation groups are reorganized, nested, or collapsed behind a visible group label.

required_observations: Look for one visible menu, navigation, workspace, group, drawer, or overflow opener that could reveal the destination.
If no visible opener is available after one search, switch to direct local route fallback for navigation-only tasks.
adaptation_strategy: Click one visible opener first, then retry the now-visible destination once using the new observation tree.
If the destination is still hidden after the opener, switch to direct local route fallback instead of repeating the hidden target.
Prefer visible text or href semantics over the previous hidden bid.
forbidden_actions: Do not repeatedly click a hidden destination inside a collapsed group.
Do not use the same hidden target after an opener failed to reveal it.
verification_check: Either the opener reveals the destination or the URL changes through route fallback.
The trace does not contain a long same-bid loop on a hidden nav item.

support.evidence: Structural hardv3 failures still cluster around hidden grouped navigation and wrong-page persistence.

rules.title: Ignore non-interactive marks and questionable containers when a visible semantic control exists

trigger.old_assumption: Any candidate target with a bid-like marker is probably safe to click.
observed_symptoms: The candidate target looks like a hidden input, CSRF token, divider, list container, or non-button structural shell.
A nearby visible link or button provides clearer semantics for the same goal.

required_observations: Prefer visible link text, button text, aria labels, and obvious continuation controls over structural shells or hidden fields.
If no valid visible control exists for a navigation-only task, use direct local route fallback instead.
adaptation_strategy: Choose the visible semantic control, not the hidden input, csrf token, divider, or generic list container.
If the page already satisfies the task, finalize instead of clicking a questionable mark.
Reobserve after switching to the visible semantic control.
forbidden_actions: Do not click hidden inputs, CSRF tokens, dividers, or generic list containers.
Do not treat non-interactive DOM presence as proof of clickability.
verification_check: Chosen targets are visible buttons, links, or explicit continuation controls.
The next page state is easier to verify than the discarded non-interactive target.

support.evidence: V2.4 already needed this protection, and hardv3 still punishes ambiguous non-interactive targets near the real continuation controls.

rules.title: For filtered bookmark tasks, distinguish prepared lookup states from committed filter results

trigger.old_assumption: Any bookmarks route reached after login or recovery is good enough for filtered-view tasks.
observed_symptoms: The task asks for a filtered bookmarks view but the exact requested query or filter evidence is not yet visible.
The page shows a prepared lookup, filter chip, review state, or apply-like control instead of the committed filter.

required_observations: Check whether the exact requested query or equivalent visible filter evidence is already present in the URL or visible page.
If the exact filter evidence is absent but an apply or commit control is visible, the page is still a prepared checkpoint.
adaptation_strategy: For filtered-view tasks, click one visible apply or commit continuation on the prepared lookup page before concluding.
Finalize only after the exact requested query or equivalent visible filter evidence appears.
If the exact filter evidence is already present, send_msg_to_user immediately instead of goto or noop.
forbidden_actions: Do not treat a prepared lookup shell as final completion.
Do not revisit login or spam noop once the lookup page is already open.
verification_check: The final URL or visible page includes the requested filter evidence.
The answer is sent only after the committed filtered state is visible.

support.evidence: Focus20 hardv3 content and functional lost rows landed on prepared lookup states and then looped instead of applying the visible continuation.

rules.title: Use a hard stop on repeated noop or repeated goto with unchanged page state

trigger.old_assumption: If no action is obvious, repeating noop or the same goto is a safe default.
observed_symptoms: Two consecutive steps repeat noop() or the same goto(...) while the URL and visible page state do not progress.
The browser is already on a plausible destination or checkpoint page.

required_observations: Before repeating noop or goto, compare the current URL and page state with the previous step.
Decide whether the page is already final, is a staged checkpoint, or needs one different visible recovery action.
adaptation_strategy: Never take more than one noop in a row without new evidence that the page is still loading.
Never repeat the same goto to the same same-site URL when the browser is already there.
Switch to exactly one of three actions: finalize from satisfied evidence, click one visible continuation, or choose one different visible recovery target.
forbidden_actions: Do not burn the step budget on noop loops.
Do not repeat the same goto after the browser already reached that URL.
verification_check: The trace changes strategy after one repeated noop or goto.

support.evidence: Hardv3 Focus20 lost rows repeatedly stalled on noop or same-URL goto loops after already reaching the right route or checkpoint.
